sections.content_assessed: Na ovom satu pokazao si da imas dobru osnovu u hrvatskom. Tvoj vokabular pokriva osnovne svakodnevne situacije. Glavni problemi pojavili su se u padeznom sustavu i u glagolskom vidu.
sections.content_native: In this session you showed that you have a good foundation in Croatian. Your vocabulary covers basic everyday situations. The main problems appeared in the case system and in verbal aspect.

items.strength_native: Confident use of present and perfect tense
items.example_assessed: "Today I am in school and I have learned many new words." — Ispravna vremenska kombinacija.
items.example_native: "Today I am in school and I have learned many new words." — Correct temporal combination.

items.strength_assessed: Dobar osnovni vokabular za svakodnevne situacije
items.strength_native: Good basic vocabulary for everyday situations
items.example_assessed: Koristiš fraze kao "Gdje je kupaonica?", "Koliko je to?", "Mogu li pomoći?" — prirodno i u kontekstu.
items.example_native: You use phrases like 'Where is the bathroom?', 'How much is that?', 'Can I help?' — naturally and in context.

items.example_assessed: "Što radiš sutra?" i "Gdje mieszkaš?" — ispravno formirana pitanja s razumijevanjem odgovora.
items.example_native: "What are you doing tomorrow?" and "Where do you live?" — correctly formed questions with understanding of answers.

sections.content_assessed: Centralni problem je padezni sustav. Kao bugarski govornik, nisi naviknut na promjenu oblika imenica. U hrvatskom svaka rijec mijenja oblik ovisno o funkciji u recenici. Ovo je tvoj najvazniji izazov.
sections.content_native: The central problem is the case system. As a Bulgarian speaker, you are not used to nouns changing their form. In Croatian, every word changes its form depending on its function in the sentence. This is your most important challenge.

items.explanation_assessed: Koristis nominativ tamo gdje treba akuzativ ili dativ: 'Vidim knjiga' umjesto 'Vidim knjigu'.
items.explanation_native: You use the nominative case where the accusative or dative is required: 'Vidim knjiga' instead of 'Vidim knjigu'.
items.evidence_assessed: "Dao sam knjiga prijatelju." → Tocno: "Dao sam knjigu prijatelju."
items.evidence_native: "Dao sam knjiga prijatelju." → Correct: "Dao sam knjigu prijatelju."

items.explanation_assessed: Mijesaas svrseni i nesvrseni vid: 'Pisao sam pismo' (proces) kad zelis reci 'Napisao sam pismo' (rezultat).
items.explanation_native: You confuse perfective and imperfective aspect: 'Pisao sam pismo' (process), when you want to say 'Napisao sam pismo' (result).
items.evidence_assessed: "Jucer sam citao ovu knjigu" (do kraja) → "Jucer sam procitao ovu knjigu."
items.evidence_native: "Jucer sam citao ovu knjigu" (to the end) → "Jucer sam procitao ovu knjigu."

items.explanation_assessed: Hrvatski koristi ijekavicu: 'mlijeko' ne 'mleko', 'lijepo' ne 'lepo'. Ti ponekad koristis ekavske oblike.
items.explanation_native: Croatian uses Ijekavian: 'mlijeko' not 'mleko', 'lijepo' not 'lepo'. You sometimes use Ekavian forms.
items.evidence_assessed: "Mleko je na stolu." → Tocno: "Mlijeko je na stolu."
items.evidence_native: "Mleko je na stolu." → Correct: "Mlijeko je na stolu."

items.explanation_assessed: Svaki prijedlog zahtijeva odredeni padez: 'u' + lokativ (mjesto) ili + akuzativ (smjer).
items.explanation_native: Each preposition requires a specific case: 'u' + locative (location) or + accusative (direction).
items.evidence_assessed: "Ja sam u trgovina." → Tocno: "Ja sam u trgovini."
items.evidence_native: "Ja sam u trgovina." → Correct: "Ja sam u trgovini."

items.explanation_assessed: Enklitike (sam, si, se, mi, ti) ne mogu biti na pocetku recenice.
items.explanation_native: Clitics (sam, si, se, mi, ti) cannot appear at the beginning of a sentence.
items.evidence_assessed: "Sam isao u skolu." → Tocno: "Isao sam u skolu."
items.evidence_native: "Sam isao u skolu." → Correct: "Isao sam u skolu."

items.explanation_assessed: Enklitika 'sam' ne moze na pocetak recenice.
items.explanation_native: The clitic 'sam' cannot appear at the beginning of a sentence.

items.original_assessed: Krivo: Jucer sam citao ovu knjigu (do kraja).
items.original_native: Wrong: Jucer sam citao ovu knjigu (to the end).
items.corrected_assessed: Tocno: Jucer sam procitao ovu knjigu.
items.corrected_native: Correct: Jucer sam procitao ovu knjigu.

items.action_assessed: Svaki dan 5 recenica sa svjesnim izborom padeza. Izgovaraj naglas.
items.action_native: Every day, 5 sentences with a conscious choice of case. Say them out loud.

items.action_assessed: 3 para dnevno: ista radnja — svrseni (rezultat) i nesvrseni (proces) vid.
items.action_native: 3 pairs per day: one action — perfective (result) and imperfective (process) aspect.

items.action_assessed: Zapamti: u/na + lokativ (mjesto), u/na + akuzativ (smjer), iz/s + genitiv (odakle).
items.action_native: Remember: u/na + locative (location), u/na + accusative (direction), iz/s + genitive (from where).

items.action_assessed: Pravilo: enklitika ide nakon prve naglasene rijeci u recenici.
items.action_native: Rule: the clitic comes after the first stressed word in the sentence.

items.action_assessed: Zapamti: hrvatski = ijekavica. mlijeko, lijepo, bijelo, vrijeme. Nikad ekavske oblike.
items.action_native: Remember: Croatian = Ijekavian. mlijeko, lijepo, bijelo, vrijeme. Never Ekavian forms.

sections.explanation_assessed: Svaki dan izgovaraj naglas 5 recenica, svjesno birajuci pravilni padez. Naglasi padezni nastavak.
sections.explanation_native: Every day, say 5 sentences out loud, consciously choosing the correct case. Underline the case ending.

patterns.native: I am going to the store. (accusative — direction)
I am in the store. (locative — location)

sections.daily_instruction_assessed: 5 recenica dnevno, naglas, sa svjesnim padezom.
sections.daily_instruction_native: 5 sentences per day, out loud, with conscious case selection.

sentences.de: Idem u trgovinu jer mi treba kruh.
sentences.native: I am going to the store because I need bread.
Yesterday I read an interesting book.
I gave my friend my book.
We live in a big city.

sentences.de: Ona ide u skolu svaki dan.
sentences.native: She goes to school every day.
I wrote a letter to Mom.
I see a big house.
She is going to school.
We live in Zagreb.
I gave a book to a friend.
Yesterday I wrote a letter.
He is reading an interesting book.

sentences.assessed: Juče sam bio u gradu s mojim prijateljem Markom.
sentences.native: Yesterday I was in the city with my friend Marko.

sentences.assessed: Šetali smo kroz stare ulice i gledali starih zgrada.
sentences.native: We walked through old streets and looked at old buildings.

sentences.assessed: Marko je pokazao meni gdje je on radio kada je bio mlad.
sentences.native: Marko showed me where he worked when he was young.

sentences.assessed: Onda smo došli do malog kafića na углу.
sentences.native: Then we came to a small cafe on the corner.

sentences.assessed: U kafići smo pili kavu i jeli kifle.
sentences.native: In the cafe we drank coffee and ate croissants.

sentences.assessed: Marko je rekao mi o njegovoj obitelji i djeci.
sentences.native: Marko told me about his family and children.

sentences.assessed: Iako je vrijeme bilo hladno, bilo je lijepo biti vani.
sentences.native: Although the weather was cold, it was nice to be outside.

sentences.assessed: Nakon kafića smo otišli u muzej jer Marko voli povijest.
sentences.native: After the cafe we went to the museum because Marko loves history.

sentences.assessed: U muzeju sam vidio mnogo intересних stvari iz srednjeg vijeka.
sentences.native: In the museum I saw many interesting things from the Middle Ages.

sentences.assessed: Na kraju dana smo bili umorni ali sretni.
sentences.native: At the end of the day we were tired but happy.

sections.goal_assessed: Padežni sistem pod pritiskom govorenja stabilizirati — fokus na prijedloge i akuzativ/lokativ
sections.goal_native: Stabilize case system under speaking pressure — focus on prepositions and accusative/locative
sections.step1_assessed: Svakodnevno 10 minuta — izgovaranje minimalnih parova s prijedlozima (na + akuzativ vs. na + lokativ: "Idem NA vrata" vs. "Čekam NA vratima"). Naglas i spora.
sections.step1_native: Every day 10 minutes — pronounce minimal pairs with prepositions (na + accusative vs. na + locative: 'I go TO the door' vs. 'I wait AT the door'). Out loud and slow.
sections.step2_assessed: Tri puta tjedno: 3-minutni monolog o tvojim aktivnostima. Consciousness insert prijedloge: u, na, iz, od, s, kod. Snima se. Slušaš. Ispravlja se.
sections.step2_native: Three times per week: 3-minute monologue about your activities. Consciously insert prepositions: u, na, iz, od, s, kod. Record it. Listen. Self-correct.

cefr_footer.summary_assessed: Nalazis se na razini A2. Tvoja baza postoji, ali padezni sustav je nestabilan. S ciljanim treningom B1 je realistican za 3-4 mjeseca.
cefr_footer.summary_native: You are at level A2. Your foundation is in place, but the case system is unstable. With targeted training, B1 is realistic within 3–4 months.

block_d.cefr_summary_native: Your active spoken Croatian is at level A2.2. You can manage in simple everyday situations, but the case system breaks down under pressure. Your passive comprehension is higher at B1.1.
top_problems_native: Case errors: nominative instead of accusative and dative
Confusion of perfective and imperfective verbal aspect
Ekavian forms instead of Ijekavian (mleko instead of mlijeko)
block_d.key_recommendation_native: Practice the Case Engine daily: say 5 sentences out loud with a conscious choice of the correct case.
block_d.next_steps_native: Focus on the case system over the next 2 weeks, then move on to verbal aspect and prepositions. Schedule a reassessment in 3 months.
